description: Legal record (#71) in a court register/pinqas from a Karaite beit din in Cairo (on f. 23v of Yevr.-Arab. I 328). Dating: 6 Kislev 5324 AM (1563 CE). Employment agreement, in which Farjallah b. Yehuda ha-Levi agrees to hire the boy (al-walad) ʿAbd al-Daʾīm b. ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Fulādī every Friday until Shevat 5325 AM. The type of employment is not stated but the daily wage is listed in two stages: 3 medin per day until Shevat 5324 (for two months) and then a pay-raise of 4 medin until Shevat 5325 AM (one year). Given that ʿAbd al-Daʾīm is mentioned as a minor and represented by his father in this agreement, it's possible that this employment arrangement was an apprenticeship. For a Rabbanite early modern document in this genre that also includes a pay raise, see: ENA NS 29.19 (PGPID 11735). This legal record (#71) includes the stipulation that Farjallah "will not let him (ʿAbd al-Daʾīm) leave/exit for the stated period of time except for the reason of excusal (בעדר // بعذر // bi-ʿudhr)." This arrangement seems to give Farjallah the authority to release the boy from employment: perhaps in the event that the potential apprenticeship was not going well. MCD.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בהספרייה הלאומית רוסיה.
